item.content:encoded: Loved the Greek tie ins, with Cassandra and the Furies. Found it kinda funny that his buddy was just straight up wrong though, unless that implies that the wrong instance won. Instance -C was probably the most depressing though. Felt bad for the poor thing. Regardless, all around, a good scip.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
That’s a hilarious take on Cassandra, I was wondering about that the minute the article mentions her

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, you got me with this one. +1, enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ha, poor guy. A short and sweet article that's got one hell of a punchline.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
It's been a very long time since I wrote something for the site, but it's good to be back.

This concept has been something bouncing around in my brain for a bit, all starting when I saw this comic by Iguanamouth on Tumblr. It evolved into an idea around what would happen if someone was triply reanimated, and took shape into its final form over the past few days. I would be remiss if I didn't mention SCP-1319, an excellent skip by Tanhony that I did not know treaded similar ground until recently, but which served as a small guiding rod for tweaks.

Many thanks to ARD, Roget, MalyceGraves, Dr Akimoto, and a few scattered other souls on IRC (including #thecritters) for helping me to refine it into this version. Many thanks to spikebrennan as well for the fantastic name.

+ Stray Observations
– hide block
  • This execution was very much inspired by the idea of a literal bodily divorce, with the unfortunate flesh being the child left in the middle.
  • Students of Greek mythology will know that Cassandra was a rather doomed prophetess of Troy who could only make predictions that no one will believe. Ioannou's friend is, in my headcanon, of the same general mold, except he can only make predictions that are wrong but extremely believable.
